Hiked lump prices by Rs 500/tonne and fines by Rs 450/tonne in April, supporting iron ore prices.
Initial Public Offering (IPO) listing on NSE and BSE on April 17, 2026. Subscribed 3.33 times overall.

Q4FY26 results preview anticipates modest growth in profit (7-9% YoY) and NII (4-5% YoY), stable asset quality, robust loan growth (12% YoY), and faster deposit growth (11.2-14% YoY). Margins seen moderating by 4bps Q-o-Q due to rate cut transmission. Dividend recommendation also expected.
Q4 loss widened to ₹211.3 crore from ₹71.8 crore YoY, revenue declined 82% YoY to ₹332.7 crore. FY26 loss of ₹909 crore.
Analyst advises investors to avoid fresh buying due to weak structure and potential to test ₹400 levels. Resistance seen at ₹460.
